description_long: This object consists of a group of six mushrooms of varying sizes, each with a rounded, dome-shaped cap and a cylindrical stem. The caps are predominantly a medium brown color, with one distinct mushroom featuring a red cap adorned with white spots, reminiscent of the Amanita muscaria species. The stems are dark brown, with lighter bases, and the caps have a smooth, matte finish. The mushrooms are arranged in a loose cluster, suggesting a natural growth pattern as might be found in a park or grassy urban area. The gills beneath the caps are not visible, and the overall form is stylized and simplified, lacking fine surface detail. The object appears lightweight and is likely composed of a soft, organic material.
description: A stylized cluster of six mushrooms with smooth brown and one red-spotted cap, each with a cylindrical stem. The group is visually simple and arranged as if growing naturally.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the mushrooms from a side angle, highlighting the varying heights and sizes, with the red-spotted mushroom partially visible behind the brown caps.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the cluster from behind, emphasizing the largest brown mushroom cap and obscuring most of the red-spotted mushroom.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a clear front view, displaying all six mushrooms, with the red-spotted cap fully visible among the brown ones.
description_view_3: View 3 shows the group from a rear-side angle, with the brown caps dominating the view and the red-spotted mushroom peeking out from the left.
